rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.

When AGREE reports type errors like '<error>' or 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace and resolve the root cause of the unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex; avoid introducing new architecture or undeclared identifiers.
Use consistent scalar types in AGREE: int for integers, real for floating-point, bool for boolean expressions.
Place assume/guarantee clauses in valid component type or implementation scopes based on whether they refer to ports or internal behavior.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have well-defined types; binary comparisons like >= and <= require matching operand types (int, real, or bool).
AGREE const and eq declarations must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
AGREE assign statements must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE unless they are present in the AADL model and configured dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
